com.mentor.chs.api.capture

Interface IXFunctionSignal

  • All Superinterfaces:
    IXAbstractConductor, IXConnectivityObject, IXHighwayConductor, IXLibrariedObject, IXObject, IXWriteableObject

    public interface IXFunctionSignal
extends IXHighwayConductor
    This interface represents an implementation of a signal. It can represent both a signal on a platform design, and a signal on a systems design. On a platform design, methods are provided to enable access to the signal's carriers, both assigned and carrying, and to the source signals. This interface does not provide knowledge about whether or not there are actually routes for the signals between IXComponentInterfaces via IXComponentPathways.

    • Method Summary

      All Methods Instance Methods Abstract Methods Default Methods 
      Modifier and Type Method and Description
      java.util.Set<? extends IXCarrier> getAssignedCarriers()
      Get the carriers to which this signal is assigned.
      java.util.Set<? extends IXCarrier> getCarryingCarriers()
      Get the carriers on which this signal is carried.
      java.util.Set<? extends IXComponent> getConnectedComponents()
      If this signal is on a platform architecture design, this method provides access to the components on which the interfaces that are connected to this signal are present.
      java.util.Set<? extends IXFunctionSignal> getSourceConductors()
      If this signal is on a platform architecture design, this method provides access to the source signals on the systems designs.
      default boolean isGround()
      If this signal is on a platform design it may be a ground signal.

        getLibraryObject

    • Method Detail

      • isGround

        default boolean isGround()
        If this signal is on a platform design it may be a ground signal. These signals are not defined in the function design and so do not have any sources. This method allows the client to identify whether a signal is a platform owned ground signal or a source signal, both of which will return the empty set from the getSourceConductors method.
        Returns:
        True if this signal is a platform design ground signal

      • getAssignedCarriers

        java.util.Set<? extends IXCarrier> getAssignedCarriers()
        Get the carriers to which this signal is assigned. That is this signal has been explicitly added to the carrier by the user. When a signal is assigned this way it means that the mapping of a signal to a carrier at an IXComponentInterface is implied. However if there is an assigned signal that is not mapped to any interface then it is not actually carried even though the carrier could be returned by this method. When called from a systems design, this method will return the empty Set. An empty Set will also be returned if this Signal is not carried by any carriers, that is the signal is only ever discrete.
        Returns:
        a Set of carriers that this signal is assigned to if there are any, an empty Set otherwise.
        See Also:
        IXCarrier, getCarryingCarriers()

      • getCarryingCarriers

        java.util.Set<? extends IXCarrier> getCarryingCarriers()
        Get the carriers on which this signal is carried. That is this signal has been explicitly mapped to the returned carriers at one or more IXComponentInterface. A carried signal is not necessarily assigned to the carrier, so the carriers returned by this method would not be returned by getAssignedCarriers(), but is being carried by it since the mapping is explicit. When called from a systems design, this method will return the empty Set. An empty Set will also be returned if this Signal is not carried by any carriers.
        Returns:
        a Set of carriers that this Signal is carried on if there are any, an empty Set otherwise.
        See Also:
        IXCarrier, getAssignedCarriers()

      • getSourceConductors

        java.util.Set<? extends IXFunctionSignal> getSourceConductors()
        If this signal is on a platform architecture design, this method provides access to the source signals on the systems designs. If this is a ground signal within the platform architecture design or is a signal on a function design an empty set is returned.
        Returns:
        a Set of source signals that this signal represents, an empty Set is returned if this signal is a ground or a source signal.
        See Also:
        IXFunctionSignal

      • getConnectedComponents

        java.util.Set<? extends IXComponent> getConnectedComponents()
        If this signal is on a platform architecture design, this method provides access to the components on which the interfaces that are connected to this signal are present.
        Returns:
        a Set of components that this signal connects to, an empty Set otherwise.
        See Also:
        IXComponent
